display.purpose: Release page headings and high-level screen titles only.

body.purpose: Helper copy, setting explanations, row metadata, and empty states.

code.purpose: Paths, model/runtime details, logs, OCR diagnostics, and developer-only technical values.

shadows.name: dropdown-lift
shadows.purpose: Custom select menus and anchored popovers.

shadows.name: status-glow
shadows.purpose: Small live status dots, not full cards.

motion.name: state-quick
motion.purpose: Icon rotation and quick control state changes.

motion.name: toggle-standard
motion.purpose: Toggle thumb movement and compact release controls.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Compact filled action for install, save, add, and confirm actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; gap: 8px; align-items: center; min-height: 32px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid rgb(35 214 207 / 0.72); border-radius: 5px; color: #f3fffd; background: linear-gradient(180deg, #28bdb7, #168f8b); box-shadow: 0 8px 22px rgb(35 214 207 / 0.14); font: 700 0.78rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-btn-primary svg { width: 15px; height: 15px; fill: none; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 2; stroke-linecap: round; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { border-color: #23d6cf; filter: brightness(1.04); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgb(35 214 207 / 0.18), 0 8px 22px rgb(35 214 207 / 0.14); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quiet bordered action for cancel, clear, refresh, and secondary navigation.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { min-height: 32px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid rgb(75 91 107 / 0.72); border-radius: 5px; color: #d7dde7; background: rgb(13 22 29 / 0.92); box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgb(255 255 255 / 0.04) inset; font: 700 0.78rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: rgb(106 124 143 / 0.86); background: rgb(17 28 37 / 0.94); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #23d6cf; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgb(35 214 207 / 0.14), 0 1px 0 rgb(255 255 255 / 0.04) inset; }

components.name: Release Panel
components.description: Flat-by-default product surface for settings, models, profiles, and history.
components.css: .ds-panel { width: 320px; padding: 12px 14px; border: 1px solid rgb(75 91 107 / 0.4); border-radius: 6px; color: #d7dde7; background: linear-gradient(145deg, rgb(17 27 35 / 0.84), rgb(13 21 28 / 0.9)); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-panel header { display: flex; gap: 10px; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-panel header span { width: 9px; height: 9px; border-radius: 999px; background: #28d6b7; box-shadow: 0 0 18px rgb(40 214 183 / 0.42); } .ds-panel h2 { margin: 0; color: #f1f4f8; font-size: 0.92rem; } .ds-panel p { margin: 0; color: #9ca8b6; font-size: 0.82rem; line-height: 1.45; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Dark compact field for release forms and profile modals.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 6px; color: #9faab6; font: 650 0.74rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-field input { width: 100%; min-height: 36px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid rgb(75 91 107 / 0.72); border-radius: 5px; color: #d7dde7; background: rgb(13 22 29 / 0.92); box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgb(255 255 255 / 0.04) inset; font: 400 0.82rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-field input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #23d6cf; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgb(35 214 207 / 0.14), 0 1px 0 rgb(255 255 255 / 0.04) inset; }

components.name: Sidebar Navigation Item
components.description: Stable left-rail navigation with a cyan location marker.
components.css: .ds-nav { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 28px minmax(0, 1fr); gap: 10px; align-items: center; width: 164px; min-height: 44px; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 6px; color: #b6c0cc; background: transparent; font: 650 0.86rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; text-align: left; } .ds-nav svg { width: 22px; height: 22px; fill: none; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 2; stroke-linecap: round; } .ds-nav:hover { background: rgb(255 255 255 / 0.03); } .ds-nav.active { color: #f5f7fb; border-left: 2px solid #23d6cf; background: linear-gradient(90deg, rgb(52 65 78 / 0.72), rgb(28 38 49 / 0.84)); } .ds-nav.active svg { color: #23d6cf; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Compact metadata token for model, runtime, OCR, and profile status.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 24px; padding: 4px 8px; border: 1px solid rgb(75 91 107 / 0.52); border-radius: 5px; color: #9ca8b6; background: rgb(13 22 29 / 0.62); font: 650 0.7rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-chip.success { color: #6ee6c9; border-color: rgb(40 214 183 / 0.5); background: rgb(40 214 183 / 0.08); } .ds-chip.warning { color: #ffcf99; border-color: rgb(255 143 57 / 0.38); background: rgb(255 143 57 / 0.16); }

narrative.overview:
VoxType should feel like a compact, trustworthy Windows utility that has been tuned for everyday setup confidence. The visual system is dark, restrained, and work-focused: it uses tonal panels, precise borders, small type, and cyan status accents to make local dictation feel ready without making the user manage internals.

The release UI is beginner-friendly first. It should surface setup readiness, model state, hotkeys, profiles, and history in calm language, while dense diagnostics remain behind developer mode. The design must stay Windows-first and must not become a macOS clone: no traffic-light metaphors, frosted floating chrome, Apple-like settings mimicry, or generic AI SaaS polish.
keyCharacteristics: Cyan accent used for action, focus, active navigation, and local-ready status.
Flat and layered depth through borders, tonal surfaces, and rare overlays.
Square-to-small-radius Windows geometry, never pill-heavy except toggles and status capsules.
Friendly setup language on release surfaces, precise diagnostics only in developer mode.

rules.name: The Cyan Is Status Rule
rules.body: Cyan is for action, focus, active location, and ready state. Do not spray it across decorative shapes.

rules.name: The Tinted Dark Rule
rules.body: Never use pure black or pure white. VoxType darks should stay blue-green tinted; text should stay slightly softened.

rules.name: The Utility Scale Rule
rules.body: Keep release surfaces compact. Do not use marketing-scale type inside product panels.

rules.name: The Plain Label Rule
rules.body: Labels should name behavior, not implementation, unless the user is in developer mode.

rules.name: The Flat First Rule
rules.body: A normal panel is not lifted. It is separated by a border and a tonal background.

rules.name: The Overlay Earns Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are for things that float above the workbench: menus, modals, tooltips, toasts.

dos: Do keep the primary release experience beginner-friendly first, with expert diagnostics behind developer mode.
Do use cyan for action, focus, active state, and local-ready status.
Do explain setup blockers in plain language before exposing paths, runtimes, or helper internals.
Do keep panels flat by default, separated by borders and tonal dark surfaces.
Do use lucide icons inside buttons where recognition improves scanning.
Do keep the UI Windows-first and practical, especially around hotkeys, tray behavior, models, profiles, and insertion modes.
donts: Don't make VoxType look or feel like a macOS clone. No traffic-light chrome, translucent macOS-style panels, or Apple-like settings mimicry.
Don't use generic AI SaaS styling, gamer utility aesthetics, neon cyberpunk dashboards, or sterile enterprise admin pages.
Don't use decorative glassmorphism, gradient text, bokeh/orb backgrounds, or marketing hero patterns inside the product UI.
Don't expose raw OCR text, runtime executable paths, VAD thresholds, prompt previews, or low-level logs in the default release surface.

Don't use colored side-stripe borders greater than 1px on cards, list items, alerts, or callouts. Active navigation may use the existing 2px cyan leading edge because it marks location, not decoration.
Don't add an in-app record button to the release home. VoxType recording is driven by global hotkeys and the target-app workflow.
